perturbation.original_text: 7.3 The Terminal Parties shall each render invoices to the CBP for any O&M Costs incurred as outlined in Paragraph 6 not more frequently than quarterly and by the tenth (10th) day of the appropriate month in accordance with procedures to be established by the F&ASC and approved by the Management Committee. The Party rendering an invoice shall furnish such further details of such invoice as the other Parties may reasonably require. On the basis of such invoices, the CBP shall pay such amounts as may be owed sixty (60) days from the 10th calendar day of the month in which the invoice was rendered or on the following working day if day sixty (60) is not a working day in the CBP's Country.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.3 The Terminal Parties shall each render invoices to the CBP for any O&M Costs incurred as outlined in Paragraph 6 not more frequently than quarterly and by the tenth (10th) day of the appropriate month in accordance with procedures to be established by the F&ASC and approved by the Management Committee. The Party rendering an invoice shall furnish such further details of such invoice as the other Parties may reasonably require. On the basis of such invoices, the CBP shall pay such amounts as may be owed thirty (30) days from the 10th calendar day of the month in which the invoice was rendered or on the following working day if day thirty (30) is not a working day in the CBP's Country.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 7.3 modifies the payment deadline for O&M Costs invoices from sixty (60) days to thirty (30) days. This directly conflicts with the payment term for capital costs outlined in section 7.4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.4 The Terminal Parties shall each render invoices to the CBP for any Capital Costs incurred as outlined in Paragraph 5 (except Terminal Station cost) not more frequently than quarterly and by the tenth (10th) day of the appropriate month in accordance with procedures to be established by the F&ASC and approved by the Management Committee. The Party rendering an invoice shall furnish such further details of such invoice as the other Parties may reasonably require. On the basis of such invoices, the CBP shall pay such amounts as may be owed sixty (60) days from the 10th calendar day of the month in which the invoice was rendered or on the following working day if day sixty (60) is not a working day in the CBP's Country.
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 7.3 alters the payment deadline for O&M Costs invoices from sixty (60) days to thirty (30) days. Section 7.4 stipulates a sixty (60) day payment term for capital costs. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict in payment terms within the contract, violating the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction: 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'. Thus the changed text contradicts the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: 7.5 Unless the Management Committee authorizes changes to the procedure for the rendering of bills associated with the Capital Costs or O&M Costs, the CBP shall promptly render bills, in accordance with this Paragraph 7 and the billing and payment procedures established by the F&ASC and approved by the Management Committee, to each of the Parties for such Parties' pro rata shares of these costs. Such bills shall be rendered by the CBP not more frequently than quarterly and shall contain a reasonable amount of detail to substantiate them. On the basis of such bills, each Party shall pay the CBP, such amounts as may be owed by the end of the calendar month following the calendar month in which the bill was rendered, on the date specified by the CBP.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.5 Unless the Management Committee authorizes changes to the procedure for the rendering of bills associated with the Capital Costs or O&M Costs, the CBP shall promptly render bills, in accordance with this Paragraph 7 and the billing and payment procedures established by the F&ASC and approved by the Management Committee, to each of the Parties for such Parties' pro rata shares of these costs. Such bills shall be rendered by the CBP not more frequently than quarterly and shall contain a reasonable amount of detail to substantiate them. On the basis of such bills, each Party shall pay the CBP, such amounts as may be owed within 15 days of the invoice date, on the date specified by the CBP.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation in section 7.5 changes the payment deadline to "within 15 days of the invoice date". This creates a contradiction because section 7.3 and 7.4 state different payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.3 The Terminal Parties shall each render invoices to the CBP for any O&M Costs incurred as outlined in Paragraph 6 not more frequently than quarterly and by the tenth (10th) day of the appropriate month in accordance with procedures to be established by the F&ASC and approved by the Management Committee. The Party rendering an invoice shall furnish such further details of such invoice as the other Parties may reasonably require. On the basis of such invoices, the CBP shall pay such amounts as may be owed thirty (30) days from the 10th calendar day of the month in which the invoice was rendered or on the following working day if day thirty (30) is not a working day in the CBP's Country.
perturbation.justification: The original text in 7.5 specified payment was due by the end of the calendar month following the invoice month. The changed text in 7.5 alters this payment deadline to 'within 15 days of the invoice date'. This directly contradicts the payment terms outlined in section 7.3, which states that payment should be made 'thirty (30) days from the 10th calendar day of the month in which the invoice was rendered'. This discrepancy creates a clear contradiction regarding payment timelines, as the two sections now impose different and incompatible deadlines. Thus, it is an 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
